title: Worked Example - Styling a Web Page

content.problemStatement:
This code demonstrates how to use the four basic CSS selectors to style a web page.

**How It Works**

* **Universal Selector** (`*`): The `*` rule sets a base font and removes default margins and padding from **every element**.
* **Element Selector** (`p`): The `p` rule styles all paragraph tags with a base color and margin.
* **ID Selector** (`#main-title`): The `#main-title` rule styles only the unique `` tag.
* **Class Selector** (`.highlight`): The `.highlight` rule applies the same style (background and bold font) to both a full paragraph and a `` tag.

---
**What to Try**

* Change the `background-color` of `.highlight` to `lightblue` to see it change in both places.
* Add a new `` tag to the body to see it get the same styles as the others.
* Add `class="highlight"` to the `` tag to see the class and ID styles combine.
* Create a new class and apply it to an element.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 100$
- $1 \leq N \leq 100$
- $1 \leq M \leq N\cdot(N-1)/2$
- $1 \leq u_i, v_i \leq N$
- $u_i \neq v_i$ for each $1 \leq i \leq M$.
- The sum of $N$ over all test cases won't exceed $100$.
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases.
- Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.
    - The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers $N$ and $M$ â€” the number of vertices and edges of the graph, respectively.
    - The next $M$ lines describe the edges. The $i$-th of these $M$ lines contains two space-separated integers $u_i$ and $v_i$, denoting an edge between $u_i$ and $v_i$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the number of good subgraphs of $G$, modulo $10^9 + 7$.
